topNews.title: Show HN: Gemini can now natively embed video, so I built sub-second video search
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Gemini Embedding 2 can project raw video directly into a 768-dimensional vector space alongside text. No transcription, no frame captioning, no intermediate text. A query like "green car cutting me off" is directly comparable to a 30-second video clip at the vector level. I used this to bu…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Gemini Embedding 2 now allows native video embedding into a vector space for efficient search.  
• Why it matters: This innovation eliminates the need for transcription and enhances video search capabilities significantly.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this technology for advanced video analytics and improved user experience in content discovery.

topNews.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the purchase. That shift, from assistance to execution,…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Digital agents are evolving from providing assistance to executing complex tasks like booking travel itineraries.  
• Why it matters: This shift enhances user experience by streamlining decision-making and automating purchases.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can develop advanced digital agents to capture market share in travel and personalized services.

topNews.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
topNews.rawTextSnippet: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit ChatGPT in droves. People marched through London in the biggest protest against AI to date. If you’re kee…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Anthropic and the Pentagon clashed over AI weaponization, while OpenAI secured a controversial deal.  
• Why it matters: The backlash against AI is growing, highlighted by significant user attrition and public protests.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can explore ethical AI solutions and transparency to address public concerns and regain trust.

topNews.title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
topNews.rawTextSnippet: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Databricks acquired two startups, Antimatter and SiftD.ai, to enhance its AI security product.  
• Why it matters: This move strengthens Databricks' position in the competitive AI security market amid significant funding.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can explore partnerships or integrations with Databricks' new AI security offerings to enhance their own solutions.

topNews.summary:

topNews.title: OpenAI ends Disney partnership as it closes Sora video-making app
topNews.rawTextSnippet: OpenAI ends Disney partnership as it closes Sora video-making app
topNews.summary:
• What happened: OpenAI has terminated its partnership with Disney and shut down the Sora video-making app.  
• Why it matters: This move signals a shift in OpenAI's strategic focus and potential challenges in collaboration with major media companies.  
• Business opportunity: Companies in the video production space may capitalize on the gap left by Sora's closure to innovate and attract new users.

topNews.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window cleaning drones and power washing robots.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Lucid Bots secured $20M in funding to expand production of its window-washing drones.  
• Why it matters: The investment responds to a significant increase in demand for automated cleaning solutions.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for growth in the robotics market, particularly in automated maintenance services.

topNews.title: From Zip to Nought: The Rise and Fall of Iomega
topNews.rawTextSnippet: From Zip to Nought: The Rise and Fall of Iomega
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Iomega, once a leader in data storage with its Zip drives, has seen a significant decline in relevance.  
• Why it matters: The company's fall highlights the rapid changes in technology and consumer preferences in the data storage industry.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for new startups to innovate in data storage solutions that adapt to current market demands.

topNews.title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Kleiner Perkins raised $3.5 billion, allocating $1 billion for early-stage AI startups and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth.  
• Why it matters: This significant investment underscores the increasing confidence in AI's potential to drive innovation and economic growth.  
• Business opportunity: Startups in the AI sector can leverage this funding to scale operations and enhance product development.

topNews.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
topNews.rawTextSnippet: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A Kentucky woman declined a $26 million offer from a major AI company to convert her farm into a data center.  
• Why it matters: This decision highlights the tension between agricultural land use and the growing demand for data infrastructure.  
• Business opportunity: There may be potential for other companies to explore alternative sites for data centers that align with community values.

research.title: Off-Policy Value-Based Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models
research.rawTextSnippet: Improving data utilization efficiency is critical for scaling reinforcement learning (RL) for long-horizon tasks where generating trajectories is expensive. However, the dominant RL methods for LLMs are largely on-policy: they update each batch of data only once, discard it, and then collect fresh s…

research.title: Leveraging LLMs and Social Media to Understand User Perception of Smartphone-Based Earthquake Early Warnings
research.rawTextSnippet: Android's Earthquake Alert (AEA) system provided timely early warnings to millions during the Mw 6.2 Marmara Ereglisi, Türkiye earthquake on April 23, 2025. This event, the largest in the region in 25 years, served as a critical real-world test for smartphone-based Earthquake Early Warning (EEW) sys…

research.title: MedObvious: Exposing the Medical Moravec's Paradox in VLMs via Clinical Triage
research.rawTextSnippet: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly used for tasks like medical report generation and visual question answering. research.title: VISion On Request: Enhanced VLLM efficiency with sparse, dynamically selected, vision-language interactions
research.rawTextSnippet: Existing approaches for improving the efficiency of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) are largely based on the concept of visual token reduction. This approach, however, creates an information bottleneck that impairs performance, especially on challenging tasks that require fine-grained understan…

research.title: Failure of contextual invariance in gender inference with large language models
research.rawTextSnippet: Standard evaluation practices assume that large language model (LLM) outputs are stable under contextually equivalent formulations of a task. Here, we test this assumption in the setting of gender inference. Using a controlled pronoun selection task, we introduce minimal, theoretically uninformative…

dailyBrief: In a significant advancement, Gemini now allows for native video embedding, enabling sub-second video search capabilities that could revolutionize content discovery and advertising strategies. Meanwhile, the concept of agentic commerce, which emphasizes truth and context, is gaining traction, suggesting that businesses must adapt to more transparent and context-driven consumer interactions. The AI Hype Index indicates a shift as AI technologies are increasingly viewed through a lens of competition and urgency, prompting companies to innovate rapidly. In a strategic move, Databricks has acquired two startups to bolster its new AI security product, highlighting the growing importance of security in AI applications. Additionally, the landscape of software contracts is evolving, with AI-driven tools resulting in shorter, more efficient agreements, reflecting a broader trend towards streamlined business processes.
